one_time_description: Ministry of Indigenous Affairs funding recovery of $85,000 recorded as other item reducing annual surplus
economic_notes: First Nation owns 16.67% of Miitigoog Limited Partnership. Keniis Development Corporation is a fully consolidated subsidiary. CMHC housing projects provide housing to low income individuals under Section 56.1 of the National Housing Act.
notes: Province is Ontario (MNP office in Fort Frances, ON). Keniis Development Corporation is fully consolidated. Annual surplus is a deficit of $985,937 including a $85,000 Ministry of Indigenous Affairs funding recovery. Accumulated surplus on balance sheet is $19,088,201 but Statement of Changes shows ending balance of $19,296,326 — discrepancy likely due to remeasurement gains reclassification ($208,125 difference; balance sheet figure used). Long-term debt increased significantly from $2.48M to $5.34M due to new RBC loan ($1.64M) and new CMHC mortgage ($1.33M). No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements. expenses_health combines Health Programs ($151,795), Health Canada Integrated Services ($415,956), and Aboriginal Healing and Wellness ($71,066). expenses_housing combines CMHC Rental Housing ($395,955), Multi-Unit Housing ($78,231), and Band Housing Projects ($124,341). total_government_transfers = ISC $5,885,111 + CMHC $271,848 + provincial $557,395 = $6,714,354. Prior year auditor was BDO Canada LLP before merger with MNP LLP.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
